rationale: Both papers study language effects in online consumer communication (citing note: textual paralanguage in social media; cited note: consensus wording in WOM), so this could be a conceptual prior-work citation rather than a purely incidental mention. But the matched evidence is only a DOI-level reference entry with no in-text citation context, and the focal constructs differ substantially (TPL classifier/sentiment prediction vs weak-tie consensus persuasion). Given this sparse evidence, it cannot be confidently dropped, yet clear substantive dependence is not established.
